\documentclass{beamer}
\usepackage{slidesonnet}

\begin{document}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Introduction}
  Welcome to graph theory.

  \say{Welcome to this lecture on graph theory. Today we cover the basics.}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Euler's Theorem}
  $\sum_{v \in V} \deg(v) = 2|E|$

  \say[voice=alice, pace=slow]{Euler's handshaking theorem tells us that the sum of all vertex degrees equals twice the number of edges.}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Nested Braces}
  This has \textbf{bold} text.

  \say{This frame has \textbf{bold} and {nested braces} in the text.}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Title Card}
  \silent
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Hidden}
  \slidesonnetskip
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Unannotated}
  No annotation here.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Overlay Frame}
  First point.
  \say{This is the first sub-slide.}
  \pause
  Second point.
  \say[2]{This is the second sub-slide.}
  \pause
  Third point.
  \say[slide=3]{This is the third sub-slide.}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Overlay Bare Number with Voice}
  First.
  \say{Introduction to overlays.}
  \pause
  Second.
  \say[2, voice=alice]{Alice narrates the second part.}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Overlay Silent Sub-slide}
  Step one.
  \say{Only the first sub-slide is narrated.}
  \pause
  Step two is silent.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Overlay Skip}
  \slidesonnetskip
  \pause
  This frame is skipped entirely.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}
  \frametitle{Overlay Silent}
  \silent
  \pause
  All sub-slides are silent.
\end{frame}

\end{document}
